top_signals.title: Shipowners Offer Huge Bonuses to Get Crews to Sail Hormuz
top_signals.summary: One shipowner is attempting to coax crews to sail through the Strait of Hormuz by offering them an extra six months pay if they’re willing to cross the vital oil channel, offering seafarers a stark choice between large financial rewards and the risk of death.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Houthis Declare Naval Blockade on Saudi Arabia as Red Sea Crisis Deepens
top_signals.summary: Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthis declared an immediate naval blockade on Saudi Arabia on Monday, Reuters reported, bringing the U.S.-Iran war officially to the southern entrance of the Red Sea and threatening an export route used by Riyadh to bypass disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.  The group said the “maritime embargo” was retaliation for what it described as a Saudi siege of Yemen, Reuters reported. Saudi Arabia had not responded to the announcement as of the time of writing. Iran had previously instructed the Houthis to…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US renews strikes on Iran after at least two military personnel killed by Iranian attack - Reuters
top_signals.summary: US renews strikes on Iran after at least two military personnel killed by Iranian attack  Reuters

top_signals.title: Latin America Outperforms as Emerging Market Currencies Gain
top_signals.summary: Emerging-market currencies gained to start the week, with Latin American names getting a boost from a drop in volatility and carry appeal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Ebola death toll rises to 930, as outbreak spreads at record pace
top_signals.summary: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention and partners plan trial of two existing jabs

top_signals.title: Labour has handed Burnham a blank cheque
top_signals.summary: The new premier’s MPs think they have backed a winner but have no idea what his programme will be
